name: Neuralink
description: Fully implantable, wireless brain–computer interface recording from 1024+ electrodes; PRIME clinical trial for paralysis.

name: Saluda Medical
description: Closed-loop spinal cord stimulation (Evoke) that measures evoked compound action potentials for chronic pain.

name: Science Corporation
description: Max Hodak's implant company; Science Eye retinal prosthesis and next-generation neural interfaces.

name: Onward Medical
description: Targeted spinal cord stimulation (ARC) restoring movement and function after spinal cord injury.

name: Merge Labs
description: Sam Altman–backed brain–computer interface startup pursuing high-bandwidth neural interfaces.

name: Blackrock Neurotech
description: Utah-array based intracortical recording systems; longest track record of human BCI implants (MoveAgain).

name: Precision Neuroscience
description: Thin-film surface microelectrode array (Layer 7) designed to be minimally invasive and reversible.

name: Synchron
description: Endovascular Stentrode BCI delivered via the jugular vein — no open-brain surgery; backed by Gates and Bezos.

name: Neuros Medical
description: Altius high-frequency nerve block system for chronic post-amputation pain.

name: Kernel
description: Kernel Flow — a wearable time-domain fNIRS headset for non-invasive brain activity measurement.

name: Cala Health
description: Wrist-worn transcutaneous afferent patterned stimulation (Cala kIQ) for essential tremor.

name: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics
description: Graphene-based neural interface for adaptive, high-resolution recording and stimulation.

name: Paradromics
description: Connexus high-data-rate intracortical microelectrode array aimed at restoring communication.

name: Rune Labs
description: StrivePD brain-data platform for Parkinson's, integrating DBS and wearable data for adaptive care.

name: Axoft
description: Soft, tissue-like neural implant materials designed to reduce scarring and last for decades.

name: Motif Neurotech
description: Minimally invasive, battery-free brain stimulator (DOT) for treatment-resistant depression.

name: Cognixion
description: Non-invasive brain–computer interface AR headset (ONE) for assisted communication.

name: Subsense
description: Nanoparticle-based approach to a wireless, non-surgical brain–computer interface.

name: Neurable
description: Everyday EEG headphones (MW75 Neuro) for focus and cognitive tracking.

name: Forest Neurotech
description: Ultrasound-based whole-brain interface for reading and writing neural activity minimally invasively.

name: Cortical Labs
description: Biological computing (CL1 / DishBrain) integrating living neurons with silicon.

name: Chang Lab (UCSF)
description: Edward Chang's lab — speech neuroprosthesis restoring communication from cortical activity.

name: BrainGate (Brown / MGH)
description: Leigh Hochberg's consortium running the longest series of intracortical BCI trials in humans.

name: Andersen Lab (Caltech)
description: Richard Andersen's lab — posterior parietal cortex BCIs decoding movement intent and imagined speech.

name: Neural Prosthetics Lab (Stanford)
description: High-performance intracortical BCIs for cursor control and record-setting speech decoding.

name: Carmena Lab (UC Berkeley)
description: Jose Carmena's lab — neural co-adaptation and closed-loop brain–machine interfaces.

name: Millán Lab (UT Austin)
description: José del R. Millán's lab — non-invasive EEG BCIs and neuroprosthetics for rehabilitation.
